leagueBriefing: Saturday was a day of high-stakes drama and massive upsets in college softball. No. 12 Mississippi State stunned the nation by handing No. 1 Tennessee its first loss of the season on a dramatic walk-off home run. Meanwhile, No. 2 Texas Tech flexed its muscle with a doubleheader sweep of No. 12 Arizona, and No. 9 Arkansas showed its resilience by toppling No. 6 Alabama in a high-scoring thriller to end the Crimson Tide's perfect start. From dominant run-rules to extra-inning battles, the SEC and ACC powerhouses are proving that no ranking is safe as conference play heats up.

games.headline: Troutman's walk-off blast hands No. 1 Tennessee first loss
games.brief: In a classic pitcher's duel that lived up to the hype, Taylor Troutman provided the game's only run with a lead-off, walk-off home run in the bottom of the seventh. The loss was the first of the season for the top-ranked Lady Vols, who were silenced by a masterful performance in the circle from Peja Goold.
bullets: Peja Goold struck out 10 batters while allowing only two hits in a complete-game shutout.
Taylor Troutman's walk-off homer was her only hit of the day after striking out in her first two appearances.
Mississippi State has now defeated a No. 1 ranked team in back-to-back seasons at home.
A record crowd in Starkville witnessed the Bulldogs' fourth-ever win over a top-ranked opponent.
games.context: The loss ends Tennessee's perfect 26-0 start and marks a significant early-season statement for the Bulldogs in SEC play.

games.headline: Texas Tech pummels Arizona in emphatic doubleheader sweep
games.brief: No. 2 Texas Tech bounced back from a Friday night loss by dismantling No. 12 Arizona in two straight run-rule victories. The Red Raiders' offense exploded for 22 runs on the day, highlighted by a power surge from Kaitlyn Terry and a milestone hit for Mihyia Davis.
bullets: Kaitlyn Terry smashed three home runs across the two games, including two in the opener.
Mihyia Davis recorded her 300th career hit, becoming the only active player in the country to reach that mark.
NiJaree Canady threw a two-hit shutout in the second game to secure the series win.
The 14-6 and 8-0 victories extended Coach Gerry Glasco's streak to 60 consecutive conference series wins.
games.context: Texas Tech solidifies its status as a national title contender by outclassing a top-15 opponent in Lubbock.

games.headline: Texas nears program record with dominant run-rule of Rebels
games.brief: No. 3 Texas extended its winning streak to 23 games with a powerful display on Alumni Day. The Longhorns' lineup connected for three home runs, ending the game in six innings and moving within one win of the program record for consecutive victories.
bullets: Reese Atwood went 3-for-4 with her 10th home run of the season.
Freshman Hannah Wells delivered the walk-off three-run homer in the sixth inning.
Texas outhit Ole Miss 14-6 and has now secured 13 run-rule victories this season.
The Longhorns are now 13-0 at home and 5-0 in SEC conference play.
games.context: The win puts Texas on the brink of history as they look to tie the 2006 team's 24-game win streak.

games.headline: Arkansas erupts late to end Alabama's perfect season
games.brief: In a wild back-and-forth slugfest, No. 9 Arkansas used a massive six-run seventh inning to deal No. 6 Alabama its first loss of the year. Dakota Kennedy led the charge for the Razorbacks, racking up seven RBIs to quiet the crowd in Tuscaloosa.
bullets: Dakota Kennedy fueled the offense with two home runs, including a decisive grand slam in the seventh.
Alabama lost for the first time in 26 games after surrendering 14 runs on 12 hits.
The teams combined for seven home runs in a game that saw three lead changes.
Arkansas evened the series, setting up a winner-take-all 'Championship Sunday' matchup.
games.context: Alabama's loss leaves the national landscape with one fewer undefeated team and tightens the race in the SEC.

games.headline: Sooners rally late to fend off Auburn upset bid
games.brief: No. 4 Oklahoma showed why they are the standard-bearers of the sport, overcoming an early four-run deficit to defeat Auburn. After falling behind 5-1, the Sooners rattled off seven unanswered runs to secure their second SEC win of the weekend.
bullets: Oklahoma's Allyssa Parker provided three crucial innings of shutout relief to stabilize the game.
The Sooners' offense exploded for 11 hits to overcome the early Tigers' lead.
Auburn remains winless against ranked SEC opponents this season after the collapse.
The victory moves Oklahoma to 27-2 overall and remains perfect in the new conference.
games.context: The comeback win reinforces Oklahoma's resilience as they navigate their inaugural SEC schedule.

games.headline: Huskers sweep Michigan to open Big Ten play in style
games.brief: Nebraska made a statement in its Big Ten opener, sweeping a doubleheader from Michigan in front of one of the largest crowds in school history. Katelyn Caneda provided the fireworks with home runs in both games, helping the Huskers move to a perfect 3-0 in conference play.
bullets: Katelyn Caneda's two-run shot in the sixth inning of game two provided the final cushion.
The crowd of 2,637 was the fifth-largest in Nebraska softball history.
Hannah Camenzind moved to 5-0 on the season with four scoreless innings in the start.
Nebraska's 21-5 start is the program's best through 26 games since 2011.
games.context: The sweep establishes Nebraska as the early team to beat in the Big Ten race.

games.headline: Gators' power display overwhelms Kentucky in run-rule win
games.brief: No. 8 Florida continued its dominant stretch, shutting out Kentucky in a five-inning mercy rule victory. The Gators' pitching staff combined for a two-hit shutout while the offense utilized home runs from Erickson and McLellan to keep the scoreboard moving.
bullets: Jocelyn Erickson and Cassidy McLellan both homered for the Gators.
Florida improved its record to a staggering 28-1 on the season.
Kentucky's offense was held to just two hits in five innings by the Florida staff.
The Gators scored in four of the five innings played to end the game early.
games.context: Florida remains one of the most consistent teams in the country, maintaining a nearly perfect record deep into the season.

games.headline: Florida State cruises past Syracuse with massive fourth inning
games.brief: No. 10 Florida State used a seven-run explosion in the fourth inning to bury Syracuse in an ACC clash. The Seminoles' power was on full display with a grand slam that broke the game open and secured a comfortable run-rule victory in five innings.
bullets: The Seminoles scored seven runs in the bottom of the fourth to put the game out of reach.
A late grand slam was the highlight of Florida State's 10-hit performance.
Syracuse has now lost 31 of its last 35 meetings against the Seminoles.
Florida State improved to 24-4 overall and stayed perfect in the series.
games.context: The win confirms Florida State's status as a dominant force in the ACC as conference play begins.

games.headline: Duke jumps on Hokies early to even top-25 series
games.brief: No. 25 Duke used an aggressive first inning to knock off No. 11 Virginia Tech and even their high-profile ACC series. The Blue Devils jumped out to a 4-0 lead before the Hokies could record an out, setting the tone for a defensive battle the rest of the way.
bullets: Duke tallied four hits and drew two walks in a decisive four-run first inning.
Virginia Tech's offense was held to just two runs despite entering the game 22-3.
The Blue Devils' pitching staff settled in to protect the early lead for the duration of the game.
The series will now be decided in Sunday's rubber match.
games.context: Duke's win provides a much-needed boost to their RPI and conference standing by taking down a top-15 rival.

games.headline: Mya Perez powers Aggies past LSU in SEC opener
games.brief: No. 16 Texas A&M snapped a five-game losing streak in Baton Rouge with a convincing win over No. 17 LSU. Mya Perez led the way with a home run and three RBIs, while the Aggie pitching staff slammed the door on a late Tiger rally to secure the series-opening victory.
bullets: Mya Perez went 3-for-3 with her team-leading 11th home run of the season.
Aggie pitchers held LSU to just four hits and two runs in front of a hostile road crowd.
Sidne Peters earned her first career save by striking out the final batter with the bases loaded.
Texas A&M has now homered in 11 consecutive games.
games.context: The road win gives Texas A&M an early edge in the SEC standings and breaks a significant losing streak at Tiger Park.

games.headline: Yellow Jackets outlast Virginia in 10-inning marathon
games.brief: In a grueling 10-inning affair, Georgia Tech out-executed No. 18 Virginia to even the series. After multiple lead changes and missed opportunities for both teams, the Yellow Jackets finally broke through in the tenth, capitalizing on a bases-loaded single and a defensive miscue to secure the win.
bullets: The game was Virginia's third extra-inning contest in their last five games.
Virginia hit back-to-back home runs in the second inning to briefly take the lead.
Georgia Tech's Kenley Hilleary earned the win in relief, pitching 6.2 strong innings.
Both teams totaled 14 hits in a game that lasted nearly four hours.
games.context: The loss is a setback for Virginia in the ACC race and forced a series-deciding game on Sunday.

games.headline: Notre Dame upsets Stanford behind Levitt's big day
games.brief: Notre Dame pulled off a major ACC upset, knocking off No. 20 Stanford on the road. Olivia Levitt's three-RBI performance, including a home run, provided the offensive spark the Irish needed to overcome an early Cardinal lead and secure their first conference victory.
bullets: Olivia Levitt went 2-for-3 with a home run and 3 RBIs for the Irish.
Kami Kamzik earned the win in a relief role, steadying the Irish after a shaky start.
Stanford fell to 15-7 overall and 5-3 in the ACC after the home loss.
Notre Dame improved to 11-16, successfully bouncing back from a tough start to the season.
games.context: The win is a major resume-builder for Notre Dame as they look to climb back into the ACC contention.

games.headline: Huskies edge Terps in tense 1-0 pitcher's duel
games.brief: In a game where every pitch mattered, No. 21 Washington rode a singular sac fly to a 1-0 victory over Maryland. The Huskies' defense was the star of the show, making several spectacular plays behind pitcher Morgan Reimer to preserve the shutout and extend their winning streak.
bullets: Washington has now won 10 consecutive games, though this was their lowest scoring output in that span.
Morgan Reimer threw a complete-game shutout, allowing only three hits.
The game's only run came on a fifth-inning sacrifice fly after the bases were loaded.
Maryland's Caitlin Olensky took the loss despite throwing four scoreless innings to start the game.
games.context: Washington continues its hot streak, finding ways to win even when the bats aren't booming.

games.headline: Mackay's second walk-off in two days leads Gamecocks' rout
games.brief: For the second straight day, Jamie Mackay ended the game with a home run. No. 24 South Carolina dominated Boston College from the first inning, using a balanced attack and Mackay's late blast to secure a five-inning run-rule victory and improve to 18-10.
bullets: Jamie Mackay hit her second walk-off home run in as many days to end the contest.
Tori Ensley led the early charge with a two-run triple in the first inning.
Jori Heard earned the win, allowing only one run across four innings of work.
The Gamecocks jumped on the Eagles early with a three-run first and never looked back.
games.context: South Carolina shows its depth and late-game heroics as they continue to climb the rankings.

games.headline: Baylor's early explosion stuns No. 15 UCF in Orlando
games.brief: Baylor wasted no time in silencing the Orlando crowd, racing out to a 9-0 lead in the first three innings against No. 15 UCF. The Bears never looked back, adding three more runs late to secure a lopsided win and force a series-deciding finale on Sunday.
bullets: Baylor jumped out to a 9-0 lead by the third inning, effectively ending the game early.
The defeat was only the sixth of the season for the 23-6 Knights.
UCF's Cocoa Jaimes drove in a run in the fifth, but the comeback bid fell short.
The game was part of Baylor's impressive 19-7 start to the season.
games.context: The dominant win sets up a high-stakes series finale with Big 12 implications.

games.headline: Sun Devils edge Cowgirls in tightly contested duel
games.brief: In a matchup of western powers, Arizona State held off a late surge from No. 15 Oklahoma State to secure a one-run victory. The Sun Devils capitalized on a key error and consistent hitting to stay ahead, despite a late home run from the Cowgirls' Amanda Hasler.
bullets: Amanda Hasler homered in the fourth inning to spark an Oklahoma State rally.
Arizona State's Katie Chester drove in crucial unearned runs following a Cowgirl error.
The Sun Devils improved to 22-6 on the season with the home victory.
Oklahoma State outhit ASU 8-6 but left several runners in scoring position.
games.context: The loss is a blow to Oklahoma State's top-15 ranking as they continue their road swing.

games.headline: Oregon's power surge crushes Nittany Lions in Big Ten debut
games.brief: No. 19 Oregon made quite the first impression in the Big Ten, dismantling Penn State in a five-inning run-rule victory. The Ducks' offense was relentless, launching multiple home runs to support a dominant pitching performance that stifled the Nittany Lions' hitters.
bullets: Oregon's Butler and Harper hit back-to-back solo home runs in the third inning.
The Ducks scored in every inning but the second to reach the run-rule threshold.
Penn State's only run came on a solo home run in the fourth inning.
Oregon improved to 20-7 and clinched the series victory with the win.
games.context: Oregon's clinical performance marks them as a major contender in their new conference home.
